---
title: "Color picker activity"
format: html

# 1. Come up with interesting combinations

Use `rgb()` to play around with different color combinations.
This function takes 4 arguments, each separated by a comma:
-   Red value
-   Green value
-   Blue value
-   `maxColorValue=` transparency value


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Example; just run this cell
rgb(165, 34, 200, maxColorValue = 255)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "#A522C8"
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Your turn!
```
:::



## Try to guess what color that HEX number represents:

Your guess:


# 2. Visualize that color


After installing the package "colourpicker," plug in the HEX notation that the `rgb()` function outputted into the widget.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Don't edit this code, just run it
colourpicker::colourWidget()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}

```{=html}
<style>.colourpicker-input-container{ display:inline-block; }</style>
<input id="htmlwidget-3a37973542c738640c3d" class="colourWidget html-widget form-control" style="width:300px;height:35px;" type="text"/>
<script type="application/json" data-for="htmlwidget-3a37973542c738640c3d">{"x":{"value":"white","showColour":"both","palette":"square","returnName":false,"allowAlpha":false,"closeOnClick":false},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]}</script>
```
